hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W, F, Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L, I, V) will reduce off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) binding by avoiding generic hydrophobic interactions. This combination will increase the selectivity margin (min(offtarget ddG) - SSTR2 ddG) by differentiating SSTR2 from off-targets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: strong SSTR2 selectivity can be achieved while maintaining pharmacophore integrity. However, structural failures are a bottleneck and must be addressed to enable progression of high-selectivity candidates.

proposed_changes.rationale: Increase the number of candidates to improve the chances of generating structurally valid designs.
Tighten the clash filter to eliminate candidates with poor geometry that likely contribute to structural failures.
